papers.title: Mechanical properties of Fe–Al–M–C (MZTi, V, Nb, Ta) alloys with strengthening carbides and Laves phase
keywords: B. Mechanical properties at high temperatures
B. Mechanical properties at ambient temperature
papers.paper_scope: Investigation of mechanical properties (hardness, compression, tension) of Fe-Al-M-C alloys with strengthening carbides and Laves phase at room and elevated temperatures.

processes.description: Vacuum induction melting and casting into cylindrical copper mould (32 mm diameter, 186 mm length).

processing_steps.method: Vacuum induction melting and copper mould casting
processing_steps.processing_steps_notes: Samples cut by spark erosion and ground; 30 min hold at testing temperature before loading.

structures.microstructure_counts: Dendrites: equiaxed and columnar, 30 μm/30x80 μm; TiC eutectic
Dendrites: equiaxed and columnar, 30 μm/30x80 μm; NbC granular/lamellar
Dendrites: equiaxed and columnar, 30 μm/30x80 μm; TaC fibrous/lamellar

structures.overall_structure: Three-phase matrix with NbC and Laves phase (Fe,Al)2Nb
structures.microstructure_counts: Grains: polygonal, 60 μm; cube-like NbC 1.8 μm and plate-like (Fe,Al)2Nb 2x5 μm
Grains: polygonal and partially zig-zag, 200 μm; cube-like/script-like TaC 5 μm, 1 μm

characterization_methods.technique: Compression and tension tests at room temperature
characterization_methods.key_findings: Compression YS 356 MPa, tensile YS 344 MPa, elongation 16%

characterization_methods.technique: Scanning electron microscopy (SEM) of fracture surfaces
characterization_methods.key_findings: Transgranular cleavage with very fine facets

unmapped_findings: Alloys with Nb or Ta show the highest strengthening effects at both room and elevated temperatures.
Fe-26Al-based alloys exhibit a yield stress anomaly with peak around 550°C; the peak is shifted to lower temperatures (474°C, 619 MPa) for Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C due to Laves phase.
Tension-compression asymmetry observed in all alloys, attributed to screw dislocation glide, Bauschinger effect, and notch sensitivity of matrix due to precipitates.
Fe-26Al-2V-1C shows pronounced tension-compression asymmetry (~36%) due to thin plate-like VC precipitates.

Room-temperature fracture modes: transgranular cleavage (TGC) for Fe-15Al-based and some Fe-26Al-based alloys; intergranular decohesion (IGD) mixed with TGC for Fe-26Al-2Ti-1C, Fe-26Al-4Ti-1C, Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C, Fe-26Al-2Ta-1C.
Fe-26Al-4Ti-1C and Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C show totally brittle behaviour at room temperature.
Creep properties, corrosion resistance, oxidation resistance and physical properties were not measured.

Fig. 5 temperature-dependent compression yield stresses (0,400,500,600,700,800°C) are plotted; Fe-15Al-2Ta-1C: 0°C 860 MPa, 400°C 680, 500°C 620, 600°C 550, 700°C 450, 800°C 100 MPa (values approximate from chart).
Fe-26Al-4Nb-1C compression yield stress at 800°C in Fig. 5 is ~150 MPa, whereas Fig. 8 reports 136 MPa; small discrepancy exists.
